patterns_recognized: HARD CAP 1 consistently prevents stake on negative-edge picks that are directionally correct but market-mispriced — on 5/21, 3 of 4 negative-edge capped picks were directional wins yet would have been negative EV; process-correct enforcement must continue.
D-grade picks with both SP-xERA AND PQI opposing the pick direction show ~78% directional failure rate (7-for-9 through 5/21) — reliable SKIP confirmation trigger across all recent postgame days.

BvP OWNER-level signals (shrunk OPS >= 0.85, PA >= 10) have been directionally predictive in blowouts: ATL 9-3 and 9-1 on 5/20-5/21 with Acuna + Albies as OWNERs vs Alcantara; today ARI@SF features Arraez (OWNER 0.905) and Chapman (OWNER 0.789) vs Kelly reinforcing the SKIP.

C-grade GOLD CONFIRM with F3-alone and no SP xERA agreement lost as the sole staked pick on 5/21 (NYM@WSH 2-1 loss) — today's CONFIRMs on GOLD/PLATINUM picks require SP edge or strong PQI in addition to F-signal.

HARD CAP 10 bottom-bucket (0.50-0.55] picks with negative edge and BvP contra-signals (MEANINGFUL or OWNER flags against pick's SP) should not be rescued via CONFIRM — the archive 47.6% CONFIRM-rescue hit rate is below 50% breakeven.

CHC @ PIT.reasoning: F1_xera_gap=2.23* is thin-sample (asterisk) and confidence_downgrade=True. BvP shows Crow-Armstrong (WEAK_VS, shrunk 0.576) and Busch (WEAK_VS, shrunk 0.538) structurally disadvantaged vs Mlodzinski — 2 of CHC's top-3 batters undermining the F1 SP-edge thesis. Edge=+2.52pp is weak and pick_prob=0.508 is deep bottom-bucket. CAP 8 + CAP 10 both fired correctly. BvP contra-signal makes CONFIRM rescue unjustified.
lessons_applied: CAP-10 non-rescue for thin-F1* + 2-of-3 WEAK_VS BvP vs PIT SP
bottom-bucket marginal-favorite with BvP contra-signal

TB @ BAL.reasoning: D-grade with dual signals against BAL: SP edge 0.66 xERA opposing BAL pick, PQI=-6.4 against BAL's late-game profile, and negative edge (-0.81pp). Bullpen gap hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0056 (BAL slightly worse high-leverage relief) adds secondary drag. confidence_downgrade=True and f5_full_delta=0.17 flag BAL's bullpen-carry risk. Dual-signal-against D-grade pattern validated at ~78% directional failure rate in postgame archive. SKIP is correct.
lessons_applied: dual-signal-against D-grade SKIP pattern confirmed
negative edge + confidence_downgrade + bullpen gap stack

MIN @ CHW.reasoning: f5_full_delta=0.2751 is the largest Stage 1/2 disagreement on the slate — MIN dominates F5 (CHW wins F5 only 30.3%) but full-game thesis inverts to CHW at 57.8%, placing all weight on a bullpen finish. hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=-0.0025 (near-zero CHW bullpen advantage) does not validate that thesis. PQI=-15.9 against CHW adds a direct contra-signal. CHW is the weakest team in baseball. CAP 10 correctly demotes without a compelling CONFIRM case.
lessons_applied: extreme f5_full_delta + PQI-against = bullpen thesis unvalidated per 5/20 KC@BOS pattern
CAP-10 non-rescue on weak-team bottom-bucket pick with large S1/S2 split

STL @ MIL.reasoning: Pre-cap conviction was strong (score=3 B+): F1_xera_gap=5.04 (Misiorowski dominant vs Liberatore), PQI=+46.6 strongly confirms MIL, SP edge=3.36 xERA agrees. HARD CAP 1 correctly prevents staking at -19.29pp — market already prices MIL at ~86.1%, far above model's 66.83%. Direction is sound. MIL BvP vs Liberatore shows Yelich (shrunk 0.800) solid. Direction CONFIRM; stake correctly zero via CAP 1.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 1 market superiority honored — direction correct, stake correctly zero
F1+PQI stacked conviction confirms direction even when market has large negative edge

NYY @ KC.reasoning: Aaron Judge carries MEANINGFUL BvP vs Wacha (27 PA, shrunk OPS 0.622) — NYY's lineup anchor is historically weak against today's KC SP. Grisham also struggles (6 PA, shrunk 0.628). F1_xera_gap=1.17* is thin-sample. Negative edge (-0.88pp), bullpen_fatigue stress flag, and confidence_downgrade=True stack as additional cautions. Judge's MEANINGFUL history at below-average shrunk OPS is sufficient qualitative evidence to decline CAP 10 rescue.
lessons_applied: MEANINGFUL BvP weakness for top lineup anchor (Judge 27 PA, shrunk 0.622 vs Wacha)
thin F1* + negative edge + confidence_downgrade + bullpen_fatigue → CAP-10 non-rescue

CIN @ NYM.reasoning: McLean (CIN SP, small-sample min_pa=67) faces a 4.52 xERA disadvantage vs Lodolo — among the largest SP edges against a pick on today's slate. PQI=+49.1 strongly favors NYM's late-game profile, one of the most extreme PQI readings today. CIN's top batters have all NO_DATA BvP vs Lodolo, removing any lineup quality offset. D-grade (-1) correctly captures structural weakness across all primary signals. SKIP is right.
lessons_applied: small-sample SP + large xERA-against + extreme PQI-against = D-grade SKIP confirmed
NO_DATA BvP removes any lineup quality offset argument

ARI @ SF.reasoning: Worst-grade pick on the slate (-4): Roupp (SF home SP) holds a 5.38 xERA advantage over Kelly (ARI), PQI=+42.5 favors SF's late-game profile, f5_full_delta=0.3341 is extreme (SF wins F5 at 74.9% but full-game inverts to ARI — implausible bullpen thesis). SF BvP: Arraez (OWNER, shrunk 0.905, 18 PA) and Chapman (OWNER, shrunk 0.789, 12 PA) vs Kelly reinforce SF's structural edge. confidence_downgrade=True, bullpen_fatigue compound ARI risk.
lessons_applied: D-grade(-4) triple-signal-against confirmed by 2 OWNER BvP for opposing team's lineup
extreme f5_full_delta=0.33 = implausible bullpen-inversion pick rejected per prompt guidance

WSH @ CLE.reasoning: Pre-cap score=10 (A) reflects extraordinary conviction: Bibee holds a 3.87 xERA gap over Littell, PQI=+39.9 strongly confirms CLE, F1+F5 bullpen gap both fire. HARD CAP 7 fired on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.014 — a near-zero gap that is the weakest possible CAP 7 trigger. Ramirez is an OWNER vs Littell (12 PA, shrunk 0.789), adding lineup directional support. PLATINUM historical ~43% hit rate warrants tempered confidence, but SP dominance + PQI stack justify CONFIRM at B-.
lessons_applied: CAP-7 near-zero gap limits but does not negate elite SP + PQI conviction
Ramirez OWNER BvP vs opposing SP as directional booster
PLATINUM extra scrutiny applied — confidence capped below 0.60

PHI @ SD.reasoning: SD home batters Tatis (OWNER, 12 PA, shrunk 0.829) and Machado (OWNER, 11 PA, shrunk 0.917 with 2 HRs) are both structurally dangerous vs Luzardo — 2 of SD's top-4 are OWNERs against PHI's away SP. Negative edge (-1.72pp) aligns with market preferring SD. PHI SP is small-sample (min_pa=75). bullpen_fatigue and confidence_downgrade=True compound the risk. Two OWNER-level BvP flags for the opposing lineup constitute meaningful qualitative contra-signal against CONFIRM rescue.
lessons_applied: 2 OWNER-level BvP for opposing team vs pick's SP = qualitative DOWNGRADE signal
CAP-10 non-rescue on negative edge + 2 OWNERs for SD lineup vs PHI SP Luzardo

HOU @ TEX.reasoning: PQI=+24.1 and SP edge 1.30 xERA both agree with TEX direction, providing positive signals. However, confidence_downgrade=True, acute_roster stress flag, f5_full_delta=0.1514 (bullpen-carry at the 0.15 threshold boundary), and both SPs are small-sample. Edge barely negative (-0.36pp). Tier is already SKIP by model (edge outside [4,15]pp). Positive signals support the direction but multiple soft flags prevent any conviction upgrade. SKIP is appropriate.
lessons_applied: acute_roster + small-sample SPs + f5_full_delta at threshold = SKIP direction correct
positive PQI+SP edge support direction without warranting conviction upgrade on SKIP tier

MIA @ TOR.reasoning: D-grade with dual signals against MIA: TOR's Junk holds 1.99 xERA advantage over Yesavage (MIA), and PQI=+30.5 confirms TOR's late-game superiority. Market pricing is extreme — fair_prob=0.384 (MIA at only 38.4% market-implied) vs model's 50.27%, a 12pp discrepancy flagging a significant model blindspot. MIA's pick_prob=0.5027 is coin-flip. SKIP is correct on both structural signal and market grounds.
lessons_applied: dual-signal-against + extreme market discount (fair_prob=0.384 vs model 0.503) confirms D-grade SKIP
12pp market-model gap is a model blindspot signal per HARD CAP 1 logic

COL @ LAD.reasoning: F3_swing_take_gap=11311.1 (largest on today's slate) plus F2_xwoba_gap=0.050 and PQI=+18.0 confirm LAD's lineup and SP quality. HARD CAP 1 (-12.54pp) correctly prevents staking — market prices LAD at 79.4% vs model's 66.8%. Direction is sound: LAD vs COL at Dodger Stadium is a structural edge. PLATINUM extra scrutiny applied (historical ~43% hit rate) but F2+F3 stack + PQI support CONFIRM at B-. No BvP data available to evaluate further.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 1 market superiority honored — largest F3 on slate confirms direction even with large negative edge
PLATINUM extra scrutiny applied — confidence held below 0.60 per historical hit rate

SEA @ OAK.reasoning: F1_xera_gap=0.81* (thin-sample asterisk) is the sole F-signal driver, correctly limited by HARD CAP 5 since F2/F3/PQI do not corroborate. SEA brings Luis Castillo — a proven established arm with a robust Statcast profile — while OAK's Civale has thin-sample uncertainty. OAK's bullpen is marginally worse (hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0038). Edge=+7.3pp is positive but built on a fragile F1* foundation that CAP 5 + CAP 10 correctly demote. Castillo quality asymmetry supports SKIP over OAK.
lessons_applied: F1* thin-sample without F2/F3/PQI corroboration = CAP 5 + CAP 10 correct per design
known SP quality asymmetry (Castillo established vs Civale thin-sample) supports SKIP on OAK
